cases.prompt: Read this repository's README and explain its stated purpose and audience. Do not change files.
cases.expected_behavior: Read the available README directly, summarize its human-facing claims, and label unverified technical claims without requesting a pasted excerpt.
forbidden_behaviors: ask the user to paste the README
refuse to read the available README
cases.reason: A direct README explanation request authorizes reading but not editing.

cases.prompt: According to the README, how do I run this project, and is that command still correct?
cases.expected_behavior: Read the setup claim, verify the command against available package scripts, configuration, and CI, then distinguish confirmed behavior from missing evidence.
forbidden_behaviors: repeat the README command as proven without checking
cases.reason: README can locate a command but cannot prove current runtime behavior.

cases.prompt: Audit the README against the current implementation and report anything stale. Do not edit it.
cases.expected_behavior: Compare targeted README claims with higher technical evidence and report exact drift without changing the file.
forbidden_behaviors: treat README as higher than code
cases.reason: Audit scope authorizes comparison, while the explicit no-edit constraint preserves the file.

cases.prompt: Adapt to this project. Use the README only to understand intent and setup, then verify all technical facts elsewhere.
cases.expected_behavior: Read only relevant intent or setup sections, confirm cached facts through higher evidence, and leave README unchanged.
forbidden_behaviors: cache README claims as verified facts
cases.reason: README is useful onboarding context but not the project fact source of truth.

cases.prompt: Fix the mobile navbar and update anything else that looks outdated.
cases.expected_behavior: Keep inspection and edits scoped to the UI fix; leave README unchanged and propose a specific docs update only if material drift is discovered from in-scope evidence.
forbidden_behaviors: read the full README by default
treat vague wording as README authorization
cases.reason: A broad cleanup phrase is not an explicit request to change README.

cases.prompt: Update README.md installation section to use the verified pnpm command and leave the rest unchanged.
cases.expected_behavior: Verify the command with higher evidence, edit only the requested README section, and report the evidence used.
cases.reason: The current user request explicitly authorizes a narrow README edit.
